## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi, this is Don Crowder with a video about the replay landing page. So the page that actually has the replay video on it, it's the page you're sending people through to throughout your replay promo. So there are several key elements that are on this page. The first is a headline. Next is the actual video itself. Next is a countdown timer to when the replay expires. Next a buy button and then any bonus downloads that you have will all be on that page. Let's talk about each of those elements. The headline. This is usually similar to the headline you used on the webinar registration page. It describes the webinar and why they should watch it. Then we get into the video, the replay video itself. This is actually the mp4 video file. I recommend that you make sure that you're using a responsive player for that so that it will shrink down and when you turn your screen on a on a phone, it'll just it'll turn to be landscape mode, those kinds of things. Just make sure that this is a responsive page. The best way to do that is to just use a page builder tool that is automatically responsive and it'll just happen. You don't have to worry about it. And I also recommend that you actually show the video navigation bar on this video. In other words, that bar down there that that moves along as the video plays that gives people the ability to pause and to jump forward and back. That's the key thing here. The reason why we're doing this is because we have found through testing that if you do not put that video bar on there, you get very little actual viewership of the of the replay. And so by putting it on there, by giving people control, you get a whole lot higher viewership of the video, which is what we want to have happen here. Now, yes, there's going to be a lot of people who are going to skip to the offer part and go directly to there. I'm fine with that. I'll take that. I'd rather have them do that than not watch anything at all. And so I do recommend you put that video navigation bar we call it the scrub bar and video, that thing that allows you to drag and choose where in the video you want it to be. One of the other reasons for that, if you remember going back to the to the replay promo sequence, one of the things we're doing is we're giving people time stamps. And so we're telling them at 13 minutes and 26 seconds, go here, you need to have the video navigation bar available so that they can slide it to 13 minutes and 26 seconds to be able to watch it at that point. That's why you need to have that video navigation bar showing. Next is the countdown timer. This is counting down to when the replay video disappears. The reason we do this is because getting people to watch replays is a challenge. There's no perception of urgency.

## [03:02 - 06:04]

I mean, it's a video, I don't need to watch a video, I can watch it tonight, I can watch it tomorrow, I can watch it next year. So what's the best way to add urgency is to put an expiration countdown on that where the video actually goes away at a certain point in time. I strongly recommend you do this. My recommendation is that video is available for two to three days, because this is part of your cart open period, which is usually five to seven days. And so I want it to I want the video replay to go away before with a couple of days before the cart itself closes so that we've got two days where all they do is focus on the cells promotion part of the way of the webinar funnel. Next is the buy button. I recommend that you have it appear at the time in the webinar when you've revealed the price. So if that happens at 62 minutes, that's when the buy button appears. Most page builders have the capability built into that to define when a certain element appears on the page. Now, some people would argue that you don't want to delay that button's appearance, because you're allowing people to skip within the video, that turning on the video navigation piece. But I want you to remember that you've actually already got the buy URL in the video itself. So if they scrub forward and watch the offer part of the video, they've got the URL on the screen and can be able to to type that in to get to your offer. So it's not like you don't have the ability for them to buy on the screen, it just happens to be in the video itself. Now, what we're trying to do here is we're trying to navigate that delicate balance between not showing the price until you've given them the information they need to be ready to buy, and actually getting them to watch the webinar itself. My experience is the best way to do that. Give them the video navigation, put the URL on the screen in the video, and then have a buy button appear at the appropriate time in the video when they're actually ready to buy. The other piece is your bonus download. So you're linking here directly to the attendee bonus that you promised in your attendee promo sequence. And then optionally, if you use the replay report strategy, you also put a download link to that. Now, both of these buttons link directly to the download with no other pages in between, you don't need them to opt in, you've already got their email address, you don't need to do anything else. What I like to do is to actually have a link that links directly to the PDF. Because when if you are on a page and you click a link that is linked

## [06:04 - 08:35]

directly to the PDF, it just instantly downloads. That's perfect. That's what I want to have happen because they're going to click on it during the replay of the video. I don't want them to take their attention off of the video. I just want them to see, oh, it's downloading great, I'll look at it later. That's exactly the psychology I want to have happen. I want them to be focused on my video, not on looking at what's in that thing they just download. Now there are two other alternatives, cut and paste versions of the replay download page, the replay landing page that I would invite you to create. The first one is a version where the buy button appears immediately. This is the one that you're going to send the people to who have previously watched the entire webinar. The reason we're doing this is because there are many people who will watch a webinar, not buy. And then they get an email the next day that says, hey, there's the there's the the replay they want to buy. They've made up their mind to buy. And so when they come to that page, I want them to immediately have the buy button to click and buy. But this version is only shown to that group of people who watch the entire webinar, not to anybody else. The second version is a version of the replay landing page that actually requires an opt in in order to view. And what I'm doing with that one is I'm running advertising to directly to that page. So I'm advertising people to come directly to the replay video in that advertising, I'm suppressing the people who have already registered for the for the webinar, they're getting a different set of retargeting ads. But the people who are getting cold ads, they've never seen, they've never interacted with me previous to that. It's great to send them directly to that webinar replay. But I want to opt them in as I do so so that I can get their email address. So that's your replay landing page, what it looks like, how you use it, the different variations of it. And that's the page that will make a bunch of cells for you.
